surface.name: Account setup + Send-Your-Data key + REST /logs/v1/singles via cURL
surface.selection_basis: Common questions say a one-off test can post directly to the logs API. The REST /singles guide documents cURL with a Send-Your-Data Bearer key and ends at viewing logs in the Coralogix dashboard. Integration packages are an alternate guided path.

alternatives_considered.name: Integration packages
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: Docs call packages the fastest guided send path, but they add source-specific setup. Direct /singles is the documented one-off API test.

alternatives_considered.name: OpenTelemetry Collector
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: Adds collector install and pipeline configuration.

entry_point.developer_intent: Create a Coralogix account and team, obtain a Send-Your-Data API key, POST a first log via HTTPS, and see it in the product.
assumed_prior_state: No Coralogix account, team, Send-Your-Data API key, or ingested log is assumed.
A work email inbox, browser, and terminal with cURL are available.

documented_first_success.official_milestone: View your logs in your Coralogix dashboard
documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: An authenticated POST to the region ingress /logs/v1/singles endpoint is accepted and the submitted log is visible in Coralogix (Explore, Livetail, or dashboard).
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: The submitted application/subsystem/text appear in Coralogix after a successful /singles request.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: REST /singles: View your logs, with all metadata fields, in your Coralogix dashboard. Common questions: Livetail is the quickest way to confirm data is arriving.

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: Dashboard/Explore visibility is the documented proof that Coralogix accepted the first ingested event.

prerequisites.requirement: A Coralogix organization and team created with a work email.
The ingress hostname must match the team's Coralogix domain/region.
A terminal with cURL (or any HTTPS client). No SDK is required.

primary_path.action: Sign up with a work email, activate from the verification message, create the organization, and create the first team.
details: The first user becomes Organization Administrator.
If the email domain already belongs to an organization, team join or Org Admin approval can replace new-org creation.
primary_path.success_signal: The team URL and console are available.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Wait for the activation email; existing-domain accounts may need Org Admin access.

primary_path.action: Open Data Flow → API Keys (or Settings → Users and Teams → API Keys), create a Send-Your-Data key, and copy it immediately.
details: Wrong key types fail ingestion authentication.
primary_path.success_signal: The key value is stored locally.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: With API key security enabled, the value may be shown only once.

primary_path.action: Map the team login hostname to the matching Coralogix domain and form `https://ingress.<domain>/logs/v1/singles`.
primary_path.success_signal: The domain matches a Quick reference row.

primary_path.action: POST a JSON array to `/logs/v1/singles` with Content-Type: application/json and Authorization: Bearer <Send-Your-Data API key>.
details: Required body fields: applicationName, subsystemName, and text.
primary_path.input: Ingress URL, API key, and log JSON
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Check key type, payload size (2MB limit), and endpoint region.

primary_path.action: Confirm the submitted log in Livetail or Explore (filter by application name if needed).
details: Livetail is the quickest confirmation path; Explore searches historical logs.
Data usually appears within a few minutes.
primary_path.success_signal: The event text and metadata match the POST body.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Widen the time range or check shipper clock if nothing appears.

branches.condition: Work-email domain already belongs to a Coralogix organization
branches.path: Join an existing team, request Org Admin access, or use SSO as configured.
branches.effect_on_first_success: May replace self-service org creation with an approval wait.

branches.condition: Prefer an integration package instead of cURL
branches.path: Use the guided integration package flow that sets key, endpoint, and application/subsystem.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Same visibility terminal with package-specific setup steps.

friction_gates.description: Work-email activation is required before organization and team setup continue.
A Send-Your-Data API key is required; other key types fail ingestion auth.
The ingress endpoint must match the team's Coralogix domain/region.

time_to_first_success.value: Usually a few minutes after setup for data to appear; Livetail is the quickest confirmation.

sources.title: Create an organization
sections_used: User signup and team access scenarios

sources.title: API keys
evidence_supported: One-time key disclosure when security is enabled

uncertainties.question: Will a given work-email domain create a new organization or join an existing one?
uncertainties.impact: Path may include Org Admin approval instead of self-service team creation.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: Depends on prior registrations and admin policy outside public docs.
